subject: reading
title: Grade 3 Reading — Beginning of Year (BOY) Test 1
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer.

passages.title: The Lost Kite
passages.text:
Maya got a red kite for her birthday. It was bright and shaped like a star. She could not wait to fly it at the park.

On Saturday, Maya and her dad walked to the park. The wind was strong. Maya ran fast, and the kite went up, up, up into the sky. She laughed and held the string tight.

Then a big gust of wind pulled the string from her hands. The kite flew away over the trees. Maya felt sad. She thought her new kite was gone for good.

A boy named Sam was sitting under a tree. He saw the kite land in the grass near him. He picked it up and ran to find the owner. "Is this your kite?" he asked Maya.

Maya smiled and said, "Yes! Thank you so much!" She asked Sam if he wanted to fly it with her. The two new friends took turns flying the red star kite until the sun went down.

passages.title: Busy Honeybees
passages.text:
Honeybees are small insects that live together in large groups called colonies. A colony can have thousands of bees. They all work together to keep the colony healthy and strong.

Each bee has a job. The queen bee lays the eggs. Worker bees gather food and clean the hive. Some workers fly from flower to flower to collect a sweet liquid called nectar.

Bees use the nectar to make honey. They store the honey inside the hive in little shapes called cells. The cells have six sides and are made of wax. Bees eat the honey when there is no nectar to find, such as in the cold winter.

Honeybees also help plants grow. When a bee lands on a flower, tiny grains called pollen stick to its body. The bee carries the pollen to the next flower. This helps the flowers make seeds and fruit. Without bees, many plants could not grow.

passages.title: Rain Day
passages.text:
Tap, tap, tap upon the glass,
The rain falls down on every blade of grass.
The sky is gray, the clouds are low,
The puddles grow where the cold winds blow.

I cannot run outside to play,
So I will read and draw today.
My cozy room is warm and bright,
With books to read until the night.

Then comes the sun, so round and gold,
It dries the puddles, big and old.
A bright bow shines across the sky,
And off to play at last go I.

questions.questionText: What did Maya get for her birthday?
questions.explanation: The story says Maya got a red kite for her birthday.

questions.questionText: What shape was Maya's kite?
questions.explanation: The story says the kite was shaped like a star.

questions.questionText: Why did the kite fly away from Maya?
options: She let go of it on purpose
A big gust of wind pulled the string from her hands
questions.explanation: The story says a big gust of wind pulled the string from her hands.

questions.questionText: How did Maya feel when the kite flew away over the trees?
questions.explanation: The story says Maya felt sad and thought her kite was gone for good.

questions.questionText: Who found the kite and gave it back to Maya?
questions.explanation: A boy named Sam saw the kite land, picked it up, and gave it back to Maya.

questions.questionText: In the story, a "gust" of wind is —
options: a sudden, strong rush of wind
questions.explanation: A gust is a sudden, strong rush of wind. In the story, it was strong enough to pull the string from Maya's hands.

questions.questionText: What can the reader tell about Sam from his actions in the story?
options: He is afraid of the wind
questions.explanation: Sam returned the kite and flew it with Maya, which shows he is kind and helpful.

questions.questionText: What is this story mostly about?
options: A girl whose lost kite is returned by a new friend
How to build a kite at home
A dad who teaches a class
A rainy day at the park
questions.explanation: The story is mostly about Maya losing her kite and a new friend, Sam, returning it.

questions.questionText: What is a large group of honeybees called?
questions.explanation: The article says honeybees live together in large groups called colonies.

questions.questionText: What is the job of the queen bee?
questions.explanation: The article says the queen bee lays the eggs.

questions.questionText: In this article, the word "nectar" means —
options: a sweet liquid bees collect from flowers
the wax that makes a hive
questions.explanation: The article says nectar is a sweet liquid that bees collect from flower to flower.

questions.questionText: What do bees make from nectar?
questions.explanation: The article says bees use the nectar to make honey.

questions.questionText: According to the article, how many sides does each honey cell have? Write the number.
questions.explanation: The article says the cells have six sides.

questions.questionText: Why do bees store honey inside the hive?
options: To eat when there is no nectar to find
questions.explanation: The article says bees eat the honey when there is no nectar to find, such as in winter.

questions.questionText: How do honeybees help plants grow?
options: They carry pollen from flower to flower
questions.explanation: The article says pollen sticks to a bee's body and the bee carries it to the next flower, helping flowers make seeds and fruit.

questions.questionText: According to the article, which jobs do worker bees do? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: The article says worker bees gather food, clean the hive, and collect nectar. Only the queen lays the eggs.

questions.questionText: What is the main idea of this article?
options: Honeybees work together and are helpful insects
Flowers are pretty in the spring
Winter is very cold for animals
questions.explanation: The whole article is about how honeybees work together, make honey, and help plants grow.

questions.questionText: What is the weather like at the beginning of the poem?
questions.explanation: The poem says the rain falls, the sky is gray, and the clouds are low.

questions.questionText: What does the speaker do inside on the rainy day?
questions.explanation: The poem says, "So I will read and draw today."

questions.questionText: Why does the speaker stay inside instead of going out to play?
questions.explanation: The speaker says, "I cannot run outside to play," because it is raining.

questions.questionText: In the last verse, the words "a bright bow shines across the sky" most likely describe —
questions.explanation: After the rain stops and the sun comes out, a bright bow across the sky is a rainbow.

questions.questionText: What happens at the end of the poem?
options: The sun comes out and the speaker goes to play
The wind blows the books away
questions.explanation: The poem says the sun comes out, dries the puddles, and the speaker goes off to play at last.

questions.questionText: Which words does the poet use to describe the room? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: The poem says, "My cozy room is warm and bright." It is not cold or dark.

questions.questionText: What is the poem mostly about?
options: A child who finds fun indoors on a rainy day, then plays outside when the sun returns
questions.explanation: The poem is mostly about a rainy day spent reading and drawing inside, then playing outside once the sun comes out.
